slides.title: Microsoft Dynamics 365 + Power Platform Regional Rollout
slides.subtitle: Regional Tech HQ — APAC Unified Workload Proposal

slides.title: Executive Summary
left: Recommendation: Proceed with 18-month rollout of Dynamics 365 Sales, Customer Service, and Power Platform
Investment: $3.2M (software, services, training)
Key Business Driver: Unify fragmented sales and customer data across 8 APAC markets
right: Expected ROI: 35% year 1; payback in 28 months
Primary Benefits: 40% faster deal closure, 25% higher CSAT, 20% sales productivity gain
Risk: Low — Microsoft has proven track record in APAC SaaS

slides.label: Annual Revenue at Risk Today
slides.context: Lack of real-time customer visibility; 9 different CRM systems; delayed pipeline reporting

slides.title: Why This Matters Now: Regulatory & Competitive Pressure
left: Data Sovereignty: APAC governments tightening data residency rules (PDPA, RMDP, GDPR UK).
Competitive Threat: Rivals unified on single CRM; we're fragmented.
Cloud Maturity: 60% of regional competitors already on Azure/Dynamics.
right: Risk of Inaction: Miss 18-month opportunity; competitors consolidate further.
Regulatory Exposure: Fines for non-compliance with APAC data rules (up to 4% of revenue).
Customer Experience Debt: Each contact repeated across 9 systems; errors cascade.

rows.body: 9 regional systems; no single view of customer; reps re-enter data per market. Sales leader: 'I don't know our true pipeline until month-end.'
No real-time opportunity scoring; forecasts delayed 2 weeks. 40% deal slip because handoff between sales and CS is chaotic.
Each market runs own support ticketing. Escalations take days; customers re-explain issues 3x. CSAT stuck at 72%.
No audit trail for APAC data flows. PDPA audit findings: 'Data dispersed, no central control.' Risk of penalty.

slides.title: What Success Looks Like
left: Before: 9 systems, 6+ months deal cycle, 72% CSAT
Business Outcomes: 4 months faster; 92% CSAT; 1 view per customer
right: Sales dashboard shows real-time pipeline across all markets
Customer service resolves 90% of tickets without escalation
Finance sees accurate ARR and gross margin by customer, instantly
Data governance: PDPA-audit ready in 15 minutes (vs. 8 weeks today)

slides.title: Our Solution — Microsoft Dynamics 365 + Power Platform
left: Core System: Dynamics 365 Sales (unified pipeline)
Service Module: Dynamics 365 Customer Service (ticketing, knowledge)
Automation: Power Automate (workflow orchestration)
Analytics: Power BI (real-time dashboards)
Integration: Azure Logic Apps (ERP, marketing platform, HRIS)
right: Why Microsoft: Cloud-native, APAC region, PDPA-compliant, low cost of ownership
Why Now: 18-month window before PDPA enforcement tightens (Q1 2027)
Regional Advantage: Azure data center in Singapore + Bangkok; <50ms latency

slides.title: How Each Solution Solves Each Pain

rows.header: Pain: 9 Fragmented Systems → Solution: Single Dynamics 365 Instance
rows.body: All 8 APAC countries on one platform; master data centralized; sync to local systems if needed

rows.header: Pain: Slow Deal Cycles → Solution: Sales Intelligence + Power BI
rows.body: AI-powered opportunity scoring; mobile alerts for at-risk deals; cycle time cut by 40%

rows.header: Pain: Service Silos → Solution: D365 Customer Service + Knowledge AI
rows.body: Cross-region ticket routing; AI chatbots resolve 50% before human touch; CSAT +20 points

rows.header: Pain: Compliance Gaps → Solution: Azure AD + Encryption + Audit Logs
rows.body: All APAC data stays in APAC region; compliance report auto-generated; ready for regulators

slides.title: Why Microsoft Over Salesforce or SAP CRM

rows.body: Power Platform lets you build custom workflows without licensing new tools. Salesforce: $500/month/app. Us: $150/month.
Data stays in Singapore/Bangkok. Salesforce: Regional tier costs 2x more. SAP: No native APAC offering.
Azure Logic Apps + Dynamics = native connectors. Salesforce: MuleSoft middleware ($150K+). You save $200K.
5-year TCO: D365 $4.8M vs Salesforce $7.2M vs SAP $9.1M. Winner: Microsoft (35% cheaper).

phases.name: Phase 1: Foundation
phases.description: Infrastructure, master data, user training. Go-live in Singapore and Australia.

phases.name: Phase 2: Regional Rollout
phases.description: Thailand, Malaysia, Indonesia. Knowledge transfer to regional teams.

phases.name: Phase 3: Service Optimization
phases.description: AI tuning, workflow optimization, cost reduction play.

phases.name: Phase 4: Advanced Analytics
phases.description: Predictive sales, customer lifetime value, expansion scoring.

slides.title: Investment Summary — 18-Month Rollout
rows: D365 Sales + Service seats; Power BI; 18 months prepaid
Implementation partner; data migration; training; change mgmt
Azure environment; premium support; training platform
Comms; training; helpdesk support for 12 months post-go-live
Includes all software, services, and 12 months post-launch support

slides.title: Total Cost of Ownership (5-Year View)
left: Year 1: $3.2M (rollout + training)
Years 2-5: $400K/year (licenses + support)
5-Year Total: $4.8M
right: Comparison: Salesforce TCO 5yr = $7.2M (+50%)
Comparison: SAP CRM TCO 5yr = $9.1M (+89%)
Winner: Microsoft saves $2.4M vs Salesforce over 5 years

slides.title: Return on Investment (5-Year Projection)
left: Year 1: Deal cycle -2 months = $5M revenue acceleration
Year 1: Sales productivity +20% = $2M margin gain
Year 1: CSAT +20 points = 15% churn reduction = $3M ARR retained
Year 1 Total Benefit: $10M
right: Payback Period: 5 quarters (Year 1 Q4 + Q1 Y2)
Year 5 IRR: 45% (exceptional for enterprise software)
NPV (10% discount): $14.2M
Cumulative Benefit Year 1-5: $45M vs cost $4.8M

slides.title: Risks & Mitigation
left: User Adoption Risk: Complex change across 8 markets
Mitigation: $400K change mgmt budget; local champions; pilot-first approach
Data Quality Risk: Master data is inconsistent across 9 systems
Mitigation: 8-week data cleansing sprint before go-live; governance rules enforced
right: Regulatory Risk: PDPA audits during rollout; delays
Mitigation: Compliance officer on steering committee; audit-ready checklist
Performance Risk: Regional cloud latency; user experience poor
Mitigation: Azure Singapore + Bangkok regions; <50ms SLA; failover to geographically closest center

rows.body: Unified 5 regional CRM instances on D365. Cycle time: 9 months → 5.5 months. CSAT: 75% → 91%. Go-live: Mar 2024.
Migrated from Salesforce to D365. Annual software cost: $500K → $280K. ROI in Year 1. Go-live: Sep 2023.
Dynamics 365 + Power BI for credit sales + audit. PDPA compliance: audit-ready in 2 hours (vs 6 weeks before). Go-live: Jan 2024.

slides.label: Average CSAT Achieved by D365 Customers in APAC
slides.context: Microsoft customer data, YoY 2023-2024. You: 72% today. Improvement potential: +20 points.

slides.title: What We Need to Move Forward

rows.body: CFO + CMO + CHRO sign-off on $3.2M investment; timeline commitment through Q4 2026
Export of customer, opportunity, and account master data from all 9 systems by May 31
Microsoft or Avanade to execute. Final SOW and resource plan by June 15.

slides.title: Closing: Why This Proposal Wins
left: 1. Solves Real Problem: $12M at-risk revenue from fragmentation
2. Rapid ROI: Payback in 5 quarters; $45M benefit over 5 years
3. Market-Leading Tech: Microsoft's APAC footprint, PDPA readiness
right: 4. Proven References: 3 similar-scale wins in region; all exceeded expectations
5. Risk-Mitigated: Experienced partner; phased approach; governance in place
6. Competitive Urgency: 18-month window before APAC regulatory tightening (Q1 2027)

phases.name: April 26-May 3
phases.description: Q&A, financial deep-dive, reference calls to similar customers

phases.name: May 10
phases.description: Board-level approval; sign SOW; declare intent to proceed

phases.name: May 17+
phases.description: Foundation phase begins; partner mobilization; master data sprint
